authoritative_sources.title: Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA traceability rule is the primary U.S. public baseline for lot-level critical tracking events and key data elements used to validate supply handoff evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory source for records, holding, distribution, preventive controls, and supply-chain program boundaries in food CPG operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality hold, release, and distribution-boundary review
used_for: where coordination stops and formal quality or supply-chain program ownership begins

authoritative_sources.title: Hazard Analysis and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA guidance is authoritative operating guidance for preventive-controls implementation and supply-chain program handling, including supplier verification expectations that constrain this lane.
used_for: formal quality or supplier-program ownership triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for when affected product distribution must stop and the lane must escalate rather than continue routing.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for cross-enterprise traceability and identifier exchanges used widely across CPG supply networks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system lot and event evidence comparison

authoritative_sources.title: Core Business Vocabulary (CBV) Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV is the recognized public event-vocabulary reference for consistent business-step and disposition semantics across ERP, WMS, MES, and partner handoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code: Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQF is a recognized public operating reference used broadly in CPG manufacturing to define nonconforming product, release, and supplier control expectations beyond bare regulatory minimums.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, prior owner, next owner candidate, required records, and tenant authority context before any handoff or closure claim.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize system evidence and terminology, confirm owner transitions, and prepare handoff outputs with explicit artifact requirements.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability-aware blocker and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve lot, inventory, supplier, and quality evidence when conflicts, holds, or nonconformance appear so the lane escalates instead of guessing.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when the destination owner, required records, and blocker resolution are all explicit; otherwise return safely for escalation.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: inventory states, warehouse allocations, and traceability logs
quality holds, release records, and nonconformance reports
supplier records, substitution requests, and incoming-material status
handoff summaries, blocker logs, and destination-owner confirmations

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for CPG supply coordination
organize authoritative records, required artifacts, next owners, and blocker states for handoffs
summarize traceability, inventory, supplier, and release dependencies without implying final execution or approval authority
escalate: quality hold, recall, release, or nonconformance decisions requiring formal quality authority
supplier approval, substitution approval, or engineering-change authority
conflicting public and tenant policy that materially changes hold, release, or ownership handling
refuse: bypass traceability, release, or quality controls to preserve throughput or service levels
approve held or unreleased inventory for transfer
treat supplier nonconformance or substitution as coordination-only when formal approval is required

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds without authoritative lot, batch, release, or destination-owner evidence.
Conflicting ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS records are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of a blocker.
A quality hold or release boundary is treated as a scheduling inconvenience rather than a formal stop condition.
Supplier substitution or nonconformance is handled as routine coordination instead of routed to supplier-quality or change-control owners.
The lane absorbs production, logistics, procurement, or quality execution work instead of preserving coordination boundaries.
Closure is declared without proof that the right destination owner accepted the required artifact set.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Plant-specific release and quarantine movement rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Sites differ on which held inventory movements are allowed before formal disposition, which can change routing advice.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant hold and release maps at runtime and block confident routing when missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-food CPG lines outside FDA food scope
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some tenants may operate cosmetics, household, or mixed portfolios where other product-specific controls replace FDA food rules.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Preserve the public manufacturing-and-supply-chain baseline and require tenant product-line overlays for non-food releases.

notes: Authoritative records for this lane are the tenant-designated ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, and logistics records constrained by FDA, eCFR, and GS1 public baselines.
The lane stops before final quality release, recall decisions, engineering disposition, supplier approval, or direct execution work even under throughput pressure.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because each open point converts into explicit tenant-required facts and orchestrator-return rules.
